frame_analyses.description: Surgeon's knot being formed with initial loop in progress.
Needle being placed at the starting position.
Reverse 'C' loop formation for initial throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Effective use of needle holder for guiding suture.

frame_analyses.description: Setting up for second throw of the single knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand placement observed for the second throw.

frame_analyses.description: Attempting third throw to complete knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Switching of hands is evident for this throw.

frame_analyses.description: Final suture cut to complete task.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Ends of the suture are being securely cut.

knot_assessments.notes: Completed with a double throw as required.
Successfully completed the third throw and secured the knot.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Slit is fully closed without a visible gap.

technique_summary: The task was completed within a reasonable time frame with secure knot tying and minimal deviation from marked targets. The practitioner effectively switched hands, ensuring the knot integrity.
improvement_suggestions: Practice faster hand switching between throws to reduce time.
Refine needle placement technique to further minimize suture deviation.
strengths: Effective use of instruments for knot tying.
Secure completion of suture throws with proper hand switching.
